key_insight: MoS$_2$ in a biphenylene configuration exhibits strongly direction-dependent quantum transport, enabling armchair-based current switching and zigzag-based negative differential conductance.
engineering_relevance: This anisotropy suggests a single 2D material platform for direction-selective nanoelectronic and thermoelectric devices with tunable transport behavior.
method: DFT calculations to obtain electronic structure and transport-relevant properties.
NEGF formalism to simulate quantum transport and I-V response along armchair and zigzag directions.
Thermal and thermoelectric analysis to evaluate phonon conductance and zT at room temperature.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports very low room-temperature phonon thermal conductance (0.28 nW/K armchair, 0.23 nW/K zigzag), zT peaks of 0.27 and 0.19, and an extreme current anisotropy of about 7×10^4 with zigzag NDC.
watch_item: Results are theoretical and depend on idealized pristine structures, so disorder, contacts, and fabrication-induced defects may reduce the predicted anisotropy in experiments.
paper_title: Direction-Dependent Quantum Transport Properties of MoS$_2$ Integrated into Biphenylene Configuration
